rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDMUND SHEFFIELD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice was reportedly served by the executor, who is the interested party. (2) Is the executor requesting to continue estate administration? This is not in the petition or prayer or petition caption. (3) No original statements have been filed to verify the ending balance of assets on hand. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Please file the seller's closing statement for the sale of the real property. (5) Was Notice of Proposed Action served for the sale of the real property? (6) The interest income from the Patelco Credit Union were not set forth in detail. (7) Please explain the multiple payments to Kim Schwa[r]cz, Fiduciary. (8) The Voya Annuity receipts were $8,161.77. Why was $18,660.99 reimbursed on 12/17/15? (9) Why was Clifton S. Thomas paid $7,500 for "preparing Summary of Accounts" on 10/25/22? (10) The market value of most of the securities is missing from Schedule G. (Are they all stocks the executor has not recovered? Why is the executor still holding volatile stocks?) (11) There is no informational schedule showing changes in the form of assets. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/12/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The temporary conservator's accounting has nothing to do with the executor's accounting; assets on hand as of the date of death are set forth on the I&A and form the basis of the assets on hand at the beginning of an accounting in a decedent's estate. Not all gains and losses have been reviewed yet in detail.)

It also appears that there were other liabilities, as there are recurring references to outstanding balance on the disbursements schedule in connection with payments to CareKinesis.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDWARD LITKE REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Granting Reformation Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/31/2023, as the following issues remain unaddressed (tentative ruling for 02/22/2023 hearing): 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 3) 26 CFR 26.2654-1(b)(1)(ii)(B) requires a reformation proceeding, if required, to be commenced prior to the filing deadline for the estate tax return, but this petition does not appear to have been filed prior to the deadline. Has the IRS granted an extension for the judicial reformation sought by the petition? 4) Was either drafting attorney licensed to practice law in California when the subject trust governed by California law was prepared for the settlor as a California resident client? 5) How did the status of the settlor's health, his location in San Francisco, and his concerns about the imminent death cause the omission of the requirement intended by the settlor to divide the Residual Trust into GST tax exempt and nonexempt trusts in the event the settlor was to predecease his spouse? Is either drafting attorney admitting that he caused the error?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SAM HOM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Final Distribution On Waiver Of Accounting (And For Allowance Of Statutory Commissions)
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] No copy of the proof of service was received by the assigned examiner. (2) The petition fails to address creditor claims. [CRC 7.550(b).] (3) What happened to the two LLC interests not mentioned in the petition or among the assets on hand? (4) How did the value of the promissory note go from $120,000 to $60,000? Did the borrower pay the estate 50% of the loan during estate administration? (5) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.a.ii.] (6) Attorney costs must be fully itemized (date incurred, full description of what was filed, etc.) to justify reimbursement. (7) The proposed order, in addition to being printed on both sides, does not set forth the distribution that was in the petition and also includes an erroneous omnibus ordering paragraph 7. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/12/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (If the probate referee's appraisal was incorrect, the executors should have disputed the valuation and had him correct it instead of paying his commission - which they presumably did - based on an incorrect (much) higher value. [Probate Code Section 8906 or 8908.] The character of a decedent's property is either separate property or community property, not just the "Decedent's property," which it clearly is. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSHUA KOTLER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/31/2023. 1) The address of Matthew Kotler must be identified by a verified allegation. 2) Notice must be given to Matthew Kotler if the decedent's parents' disclaimers were timely and effective. 3) The declaration filed on 04/03/2023 must be treated as an amendment requiring new notice to the extent that additional time to administer the estate beyond 12/31/2022 is requested. 4) Some information required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1) has not been provided (whether notice is required under Probate Code section 9202(b) and whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution). 5) A proof that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is otherwise held in the form of investment(s) authorized by section 9730 has not been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIEN HEDQUIST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the verification of the amendment to the petition (and paragraph 6 of the amendment) referred to the wrong pleading. (Please read CRC 7.3 closely for the distinction between an amendment to a pleading and a fully amended pleading.) The attorney costs also include a $128 messenger cost in connection with a "Supp. To Petition on 1/13/22", but the supplement to the initial petition was e-filed on 1/12/22, not conventionally filed. Status of filing the Receipt for the blocked account will be set for 5/2/23. Paragraph 4 of the prayer should have set forth the net amount of cash to be distributed, not just "the rest and residue".

Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DECLARATION OF TRUST OF ROSA E. MANLEY, DATED APRIL 29, 2013
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Modifying Irrevocable Trust
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELEANOR JUDITH DAVIS BYPASS TRUST CREATED UNDER THE DAVIS FAMILY REVOCABLE INTER VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement And For Approval Of Construction Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 05/17/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) The proof of service on file shows notice was given on 03/20/2023, less than 30 days before this hearing. New notice must be given. 3) Is Jeffrey McHenry represented by an attorney in the probate proceeding in Santa Clara County? If so, notice must also be given to his attorney. 4) Please provide a copy of a court order that appointed the petitioner as successor trustee of the subject sub-trust. If the petitioner was not appointed by any court, please state the date of acceptance of the sub-trust and explain how the appointment was effected. 5) The beginning balance of this account cannot be verified without settlement of the first account and its ending balance. Is the petitioner requesting an order merely acknowledging the account as presented? 6) Please explain why this sub-trust received distributions from the survivor's trust in April 2021. 7) Please explain why this tub-trust made distributions to the survivor's trust between January 2020 and April 2021. 8) Please explain why no distributions to the income beneficiary were reported during this account period. 9) Did the trustee exercise its discretion under section 6.4(b) of the governing trust by withholding distribution of any part of net income as unnecessary for the reasonable care, maintenance, support and education of the income beneficiary? If so, please state the total amount of undistributed net income and provide facts supporting the petitioner's necessity determination. 10) Without settlement of the first account and confirmation that the petitioner made all distribution of net income determined to be necessary for the income beneficiary during the first account period, the residue distributable to the remainder beneficiary as of the end of this account period cannot be ascertained.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARIE HOWARD REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions To Determine Existence Of The Revocable Living Trust Of Marie Howard, Dated February 12, 2003
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petitioner's response to Objection has not been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FELICIE AGUERRE FREITAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Status of closing the estate is set for 4/23/2024. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least two weeks before 4/23/24. Appearance will be required on 4/23/24 if no petition has been filed. NB: When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing -- including proposed orders -- must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.]
